channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Robinhood cofounder Baiju Bhatt wants to create the Starlink of solar power
item.description: It’s been seven months since Robinhood cofounder Baiju Bhatt entered stealth mode. Last March, he left his CCO perch at the online brokerage valued at $23 billion to quietly shift gears.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk is hampering hurricane relief efforts—and using X to do it
item.description: When Federal Emergency Management Agency first responders raced to western North Carolina in the days after Hurricane Helene, they expected much of the devastation they found in its wake: totaled cars, crumbling roads, homes filled with mud or ruined by floodwaters or washed away entirely.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive rain, winds, scientists say
item.description: Scientists say human-caused climate change intensified deadly Hurricane Milton’s rainfall by 20 to 30% and strengthened its winds by about 10%.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Spain Announces Major Digitalization Project for Cities - "Smart City" Data Sharing Initiative
item.description: Spanish government launches digitalization project to improve public services in cities through data sharing, targeting smart city infrastructure and urban planning
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why you should want face-recognition glasses
item.description: You start a conversation with someone, wondering if you’ve met them before. But then, your smart glasses tell you their name and where, when, and in what context you actually did meet them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: More than one-third of cloud environments are critically exposed, says Tenable
item.description: In the first half of this year, 38% of organizations had at least one cloud workload that was critically vulnerable, highly privileged, and publicly exposed, according to a study of telemetry from customers of cloud security vendor Tenable released this week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane’s impact on semiconductor industry remains a threat
item.description: One of two plants that mine high-purity quartz and silica sand needed for the production of semiconductors and other high-tech hardware has reopened operations after being shut down for more than a week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta AI launches in Brazil, U.K., and more markets in a bid to beat ChatGPT
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Wednesday its artificial intelligence chatbot, Meta AI, will be available in 21 new markets, as it looks to compete with OpenAI’s ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Here's what has made Hurricane Milton so fierce and unusual
item.description: With its mighty strength and its dangerous path, Hurricane Milton has powered into a very rare threat that is flirting with experts’ worst fears.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google could be forced to sell parts of its business if the DOJ gets its way
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to force Google to sell parts of its business in order to eliminate its online search monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Big Tech’s experimental AI can’t beat old-school productivity gems
item.description: The “Chat” in ChatGPT’s name neatly encapsulates a major element of why OpenAI’s AI-infused assistant has had such epoch-shifting impact. We’ve been using PCs and phones to chat with humans for years—at least since the era of AOL Instant Messenger.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Creepy yet helpful robot is ready to assist
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scientists share Nobel Prize in chemistry for AI protein work
item.description: Two Google DeepMind scientists shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in chemistry with a US professor for their breakthrough research into proteins.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Ransomware attack impacts 237,000 Comcast customers
item.description: Debt agency breach exposes 237,700 Comcast customers' personal data. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and provides six tips to help protect yourself from such a breach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Microsoft wants Copilot to be your new AI best friend
item.description: “It’s great to see you!” Microsoft’s Copilot AI chatbot underwent a transformation last week, morphing into a simplified pastel-toned experience that encourages you…to just chat.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service
item.description: The new update allows users to connect their phones to satellite, as long its an iPhone 14 or later model running iOS18.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Harris backers praise Lina Khan at tech-focused fundraiser
item.description: Some Vice President Harris backers praised Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan at a tech-focused San Francisco fundraiser on Saturday headlined by former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.).
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: As global AI energy usage mounts, Nvidia claims efficiency gains of up to 100,000X
item.description: As concerns over AI energy consumption ratchet up, chip maker Nvidia is defending what it calls a steadfast commitment to sustainability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
